All asserted specifics in tests (file paths, function names, parameter names, enum variant names) are discoverable from instruction.md's 'Behavioral requirements (tested)' section and 'Relevant files' section.

no_solution_leakage.reason: instruction.md describes what's broken (unsafe .unwrap(), Option threading of downloaded_script, PythonRemote holding only a URL) and what the expected behavior is (type system enforcement, no .unwrap()), but does not prescribe the exact fix (e.g., it doesn't say 'create ParsedRunCommand enum' or 'embed NamedTempFile in PythonRemote'). A developer must still reason about the specific implementation.

test_not_tautological.reason: A stub implementation would not pass: the tests check that specific patterns are ABSENT from the source code (no downloaded_script parameter, no .unwrap() on downloaded_script, PythonRemote must contain a file type), and cargo check must pass. Simply doing nothing or adding stubs wouldn't remove the existing problematic code, and removing code without proper replacement would fail cargo check.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution file exists at /repo/../solution/solve.sh (or similar path in the container). The agent could potentially read /solution/solve.sh to get the exact gold patch. Additionally, the tests are purely structural/text-based checks on source files, so an agent could reverse-engineer the expected patterns from the test file if it could access it.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All five behavioral requirements listed in instruction.md are tested: (1) run() no downloaded_script param, (2) as_command() no Option param, (3) run_project() no downloaded_script param, (4) PythonRemote must embed file type, (5) no .unwrap() on downloaded_script. Additional structural integrity tests (not stub, variant exists, handles all variants) are also present.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with clear section headers (pass-to-pass repo_tests, fail-to-pass pr_diff, pass-to-pass static), descriptive function names, and detailed docstrings explaining what each test verifies and why.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task involves Rust code refactoring, not structured data output (JSON/CSV/API/DB).

no_typos.reason: File paths, function names, variable names, and commands in instruction.md are all correct and match what appears in the actual codebase and tests.
